text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 63-year-old female with known stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma, right kidney primary (status post right radical nephrectomy with retroperitoneal lymph node dissection), with bilateral pulmonary metastases and previously documented hepatic lesions. Currently on Opdivo 480 mg IV monotherapy every 4 weeks (maintenance phase following Yervoy/Opdivo induction). Prior CT approximately 5 months ago demonstrated partial reduction in dominant pulmonary lesions. Restaging to assess current disease burden.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following intravenous administration of iohexol 370 mg I/mL at standard weight-based dosing. Axial images were acquired during portal venous phase; delayed phase imaging of the abdomen performed at 5 minutes post-injection to characterize hepatic lesions. Coronal and sagittal reformations generated. Dose modulation utilized. No oral contrast was administered. Prior CT from approximately 5 months prior is available for comparison.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The airways are patent to the segmental level bilaterally. There is no new pleural effusion. No pneumothorax identified.

Pulmonary Nodules/Masses: There has been interval increase in size of the previously identified bilateral pulmonary nodules.

- Right lower lobe: The dominant lesion, which had decreased to 1.0 cm on the prior study, now measures 2.4 x 1.9 cm in axial dimensions (series 4, image 112), representing a substantial increase from the prior examination. The lesion demonstrates intermediate attenuation with mild internal heterogeneity on delayed imaging. This finding substantially exceeds the original pre-treatment measurement of 1.8 cm.

- Left upper lobe: Nodule previously measuring 0.5 cm has enlarged to 1.2 x 1.0 cm (series 4, image 58). Previously round and well-marginated morphology is maintained, though the nodule is now more conspicuous.

- Right upper lobe: Nodule previously 0.7 cm now measures 1.4 x 1.1 cm (series 4, image 42). The nodule has developed a slightly irregular spiculated margin at its posterior aspect, new compared to the prior study.

- Left lower lobe: Nodule previously measuring 0.6 cm has increased to 1.1 x 0.9 cm (series 4, image 131). Well-defined, peripheral.

- Right middle lobe: Two new small soft tissue nodules identified in the right middle lobe, measuring approximately 0.5 cm and 0.6 cm in greatest dimension (series 4, images 96-99), not present on the prior examination. Morphology is consistent with metastatic deposits.

Heart and Pericardium: Cardiac silhouette is within normal limits. No pericardial effusion. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without dissection.

Mediastinum and Hilar Regions: No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes. No new mediastinal masses.

Chest Wall and Osseous Structures: No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions in the visualized ribs, sternum, clavicles, or thoracic spine. Mild degenerative changes in the lower thoracic spine.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size. Two new enhancing lesions are identified in the right hepatic lobe:

- Segment VI/VII: A hypervascular lesion measuring approximately 0.8 cm on portal venous phase imaging, with early arterial phase enhancement and washout on delayed phase imaging (series 7, image 44). This pattern is concerning for metastatic deposit. This lesion was not identified on the prior CT.

- Segment VII: A second hypervascular focus measuring approximately 0.6 cm with similar enhancement characteristics (series 7, image 51), also new compared to the prior examination.

No additional hepatic lesions identified. No biliary ductal dilatation. Portal vein is patent.

Nephrectomy Bed / Right Retroperitoneum: The right nephrectomy bed appears intact without evidence of a discrete soft tissue mass or local recurrence. Post-surgical changes are stable. The right adrenal gland appears unremarkable.

Left Kidney and Adrenal Gland: The left kidney is normal in size and demonstrates symmetric cortical enhancement without hydronephrosis or mass lesion. Left adrenal gland is unremarkable.

Spleen, Pancreas, Gallbladder: Spleen is normal in size. No splenic lesions. Pancreatic parenchyma and duct are unremarkable. No gallstones. No pericholecystic fluid.

Retroperitoneal Lymph Nodes: No pathologically enlarged retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymph nodes identified. The prior hilar/paraaortic nodal disease has been treated; no residual or recurrent nodal disease detected.

Bowel: Visualized loops of small and large bowel are normal in caliber without wall thickening, obstruction, or pneumatosis.

Ascites: None.

---

PELVIS:

No pathologically enlarged iliac or inguinal lymph nodes. Bladder appears unremarkable. No pelvic free fluid. Uterus and adnexa not well-evaluated on this examination. No pelvic osseous lesions.

---

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES (IMAGED PORTIONS):

No definitive lytic or sclerotic lesions suspicious for osseous metastatic involvement in the visualized spine, pelvis, proximal femora, or ribs. Mild degenerative spondylosis and facet arthropathy throughout the lumbar spine, unchanged. Bilateral hip degenerative changes, unchanged from prior.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Worsening bilateral pulmonary metastases compared to the prior examination approximately 5 months ago. The dominant right lower lobe lesion has grown substantially from 1.0 cm to 2.4 cm, now exceeding its pre-treatment size. All four previously documented nodules have enlarged, and two new small right middle lobe nodules have appeared — findings collectively indicating disease progression in the lungs.

2. Two new hypervascular lesions in the right hepatic lobe (0.8 cm and 0.6 cm), not present on prior imaging, with arterial enhancement and washout pattern suspicious for hepatic metastases. No prior hepatic disease was identified; these represent a new site of involvement.

3. No evidence of local recurrence at the right nephrectomy bed.

4. No new osseous lesions or lymph node enlargement.

5. Stable post-surgical changes from prior right radical nephrectomy.

Overall, the findings indicate interval progression of metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma with worsening pulmonary burden and the development of new hepatic lesions, representing emergence of a new organ site since the prior restaging examination.

Reporting Radiologist: Attending Radiologist, Abdominal Imaging Division
Electronic Signature: Finalized
